title: Socio-Economic and Ethical Implications of Various Agricultural Biotechnology Interventions
abstract:
Biotechnology is the appl icat ion of biological methods or processes to
produce useful products. The ancient Egypt ians were credi ted wi t h
the invent ion of ferment ing beer by using yeast. Indians have been making
curds by using biologi cal cul tures for centur ies. Several convent ional
biotechnology processes and approaches are in use in India.
